next_action.description: Resume at Phase 4. Phase 3 is COMMITTED (47bac3b8) and fully live-verified (incl. remote Pod). Do Phase 4: git rm comfy_workflows/input/placeholder.png + comfy_workflows/input/ltx_silence.wav (media migration is proven, the placeholders are dead), then rewrite the injection-rules docs to the path->string contract (media inputs are path-reading loader nodes with a string field; empty string self-gates via ExecutionBlocker; latents are the SOLE staging survivor — do NOT remove latents). Then Phase 5 docs: reference scripts/sync-raw-workflows.mjs (the git-driven raw->API sync) in docs/playbooks/add-model/ and add-app/. Verify the 20 pre-staged generated files before /mpi-end (the release agent moved the tree — re-check what is staged). NEVER write into comfy_workflows/raw/ (user-owned). Do NOT re-run orchestrate.py on a dirty tree. The LTX duration/timing bug is DEFERRED to the user (browser + t2v-no-audio discriminator) — not a MPI-272 regression, do not chase it unless the user brings back results.

key_decisions: Phase 3 injector routes media by TARGET NODE CLASS (MpiLoadImageFromPath/MpiLoadAudio/MpiLoadVideo/VHS_LoadVideoPath/MpiString fan-out), NOT by title pattern. The old input_image/video/audio title sweep missed the video frame slots (Input_Start_Frame/Input_End_Frame). Class-driven is title-agnostic and covers all current + future slots.

Data-URL media (auto-mask painted mask, params.Input_Mask = maskDataUrl) is staged to a hashed file in the engine input dir via POST /comfy/stage-media-data-url, then flows through the normal resolve/upload/inject path. Path nodes (os.path.isfile) cannot read a data: URL.

Audio slot title unified to Input_audio (was Input_Audio_File in 2 ops). Injector title match is case-insensitive (.toLowerCase() both sides), so the node titled Input_Audio still matches — no need to force case anywhere.

Pod MpiNodes comes from a GitHub clone pinned by commit in dev_configs/node_lock.json — NOT R2, NOT start.sh, NOT the app release. Bumped 2d409b54 -> 0391e34; Pod reinstalls the volume node on next connect (MPI-222).

Converter seed-phantom fix matches the ComfyUI frontend rule EXACTLY (useIntWidget.ts + docs/WIDGET_SERIALIZATION.md): control = inputSpec.control_after_generate ?? ['seed','noise_seed'].includes(inputSpec.name). 158 engine nodes set the flag; only flag-less seed nodes (RES4LYF/Impact/MpiNodes) needed the name fallback.
constraints: NEVER write/move/convert-in-place/git-checkout anything under comfy_workflows/raw/ — user's only editable LiteGraph sources. See feedback_raw_workflows_user_owned.

Do NOT re-run orchestrate.py on a dirty tree — global rebuild clobbers uncommitted generated files. Hash-gated per-template, but only safe on a clean tree or after re-converting only the intended templates. See feedback_orchestrate_global_rebuild_hazard.
Agents NEVER edit workflow JSON under comfy_workflows/ — contract changes go through the user re-titling/re-authoring in ComfyUI + re-export.

Shared tree, concurrent agents (a release agent + a deleted-asset-toast agent are/were active). Commit ONLY by explicit pathspec (git commit --only <paths>), never git add -A. js/shell.js + js/shell/heroStats.js are NOT this task's files — leave them. See feedback_shared_tree_commit_hygiene + feedback_concurrent_sessions_same_file.
The reuse-404 soft-error (_assertMediaSourceExists) is load-bearing — MPI-227/225 history. Losing it regresses reuse UX to the bug-reporter dialog.
Case-insensitivity already handled everywhere (injector, validator, capture law all .toLowerCase()). Do NOT add a case-linter — user confirmed no value in nagging about case that already works.
The LTX duration bug is NOT a MPI-272 regression and is DEFERRED to the user's browser/t2v-no-audio discriminator. Do not debug it proactively.

Then Phase 5 docs: reference scripts/sync-raw-workflows.mjs in the add-model/add-app playbooks. Re-check the pre-staged generated files before /mpi-end (a release agent moved the tree). NEVER write into comfy_workflows/raw/ (user-owned) and do NOT re-run orchestrate.py on a dirty tree. NOTE: the LTX-2.3 duration/timing bug (1s and 2s both produced ~500ms) is DEFERRED to the user's own browser + t2v-no-audio discriminator test — it is NOT a MPI-272 regression, so do not debug it unless the user returns with results.
